label: fiction
title: The Turnstile Twin
elements: The shop was robbed as charged.

beats.text: Defence counsel puts it squarely through their expert: the accused has an identical twin. The CCTV face-match is a match for both of them. 'That footage convicts a face my client shares.'
beats.reveal_note: True, and cleverly framed — the camera alone can't choose between them. But the argument only lives while the twin's whereabouts are unknown, and they aren't.

beats.text: Hospital records: the twin was in the emergency department across town — triaged, treated, discharged — throughout the entire window of the robbery.
beats.reveal_note: The twin defence dies in triage. If the face on camera belongs to one of two men, and one was on a hospital trolley, the camera has chosen.

beats.text: The cashier noticed something the camera half-caught: the robber kept his left wrist stiff and worked the counter with his right, 'like it hurt him.' The accused's medical file shows an old left-wrist fracture, badly healed.
beats.reveal_note: A body's habit, not a face: the kind of detail a twin doesn't share.

beats.text: Fibre analysis: tufts recovered from the counter's tape strip match the accused's jacket — the one seized from his flat, star-shaped repair stitch and all.
beats.reveal_note: Clothes are not genetic. The jacket at the counter is the jacket from his wardrobe.

beats.text: The judge: the doubt the law requires is reasonable doubt — a real possibility, not a merely conceivable one.
beats.reveal_note: The line the whole case walks: an identical twin is a real possibility only until the hospital record makes him a merely conceivable one.

twist: The twin defence is the rare trick that's true right up until it isn't: the camera really can't tell them apart. The emergency department could — one brother was on a trolley across town all evening, and the other one's jacket left its fibres on the counter.
